questions.question: Who wrote the national anthem of India?
What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 28, 30, 32, 34, 36?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
Find the next number in the series: 4, -6, -16, -26, -36, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows an Arithmetic Progression with a constant difference of -10. So, -36 + (-10) = -46.
questions.trick: Always check the difference between the first and second terms and verify across the series.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 121 km/hr. How much distance will it cover in 4 hours?
questions.trick: Basic multiplication rule: D = S * T.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
Find the next number in the series: 3, 6, 12, 24, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows a Geometric Progression with a constant multiplier ratio of 2. So, 24 * 2 = 48.
questions.trick: If numbers scale up very quickly, look for multiplication rather than addition.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Dogs are Cats.
2. All Cats are Animals.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.

questions.question: A can complete a piece of work in 112 days while B can complete the same work in 240 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
If an article is bought for Rs. 1360 and sold at a profit of 25%, what is the selling price?
questions.trick: Calculate 10% of CP, multiply, and add it conceptually.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 82, 25, 16, 36
questions.explanation: 16, 25, and 36 are perfect squares. 82 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.
